dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Ran into Artemis out there, at long last. Did she pass through and say hello at least?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No, she didn't. So she's been skulking around in these woods? Thought she was busy rallying your aunts and uncles for you. Wait, you couldn't go the distance even with her help?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Have you met Artemis out there? I suppose it may be worth repeatedly disobeying orders for the chance to see her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What would you know of it. You really think I'm sneaking out into those woods just to fool around?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Is everything all right between the two of you, at least?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Singing, eugh. The goddess of the hunt apparently is looking for her brother's job. Surprised you're not still over there yourself. Something else you could be practicing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It may seem frivolous though we don't sing merely for lack of things to do here, Nemesis. It has a cleansing quality.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, right. Artemis told me all about what you mean when you talk about cleansing. Least I'm just about out of earshot over here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Been hunched over that cauldron so much, least your craft is finally doing something around here. So much toiling with no results...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Our craft is typically about the self, and such results tend not to be immediate. Though, I appreciate you noticing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: So you've been chatting it up with Chaos, of all beings... trying to get the oldest force in the world to take sides? A fundamentally impartial force. Smart.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like you've been chatting it up yourself! I saw you plunge into a Chaos Gate, as though you knew exactly where it led. Not your first time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not your business. Just don't go thinking Chaos is going to help you and no one else. Seeing as that wouldn't be fair.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Strictly business between you and Lord Charon, isn't it? Never seen you speak except about his wares. At least you're providing him with Gold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You might technically have a brother, but you don't know what it's like having one around. Charon doesn't like to talk; I respect that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Truly? He never seems to mind speaking with me, in his way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Ought to admit it when I'm wrong. Never thought you'd get to Chronos in the first place, before I did anyway. Much less beat him down, repeatedly. So — I was wrong. Sorry.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis, I want you to know it was astonishing to hear those words coming from you just now. It seems we're each more than capable of surprising the other. But mainly... thank you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No need. Fair is fair. Had my chances to catch up with you, but couldn't get through all the pitfalls in the way. Might be I could have taken Chronos, too. But now I'm not so sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Now don't lose heart! There's much left to be done, and we need all your strength and vigilance. We're the Unseen; together we prevail, or we fail.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't believe you got to Chronos before me. Knew you stood no chance against him, but... didn't think you'd get there at all anytime soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Is that supposed to be some sort of compliment? Or an apology, perhaps?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't believe you got to Chronos before me. Knew you stood no chance against him, but... didn't think you'd get there at all anytime soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Is that supposed to be some sort of compliment? Or an apology, perhaps?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: They're saying you got all the way to Tartarus before. How far'd you get? Make it all the way back to your rightful home?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you're referring to the House of Hades, then yes, I did make it there. Though you could best describe it as the House of Chronos, at this point. He was waiting within, just as expected, and... was strong, just as expected, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Too strong, you mean. Well, can't fault you for trying. So what's the plan now, keep throwing yourself at him till you're totally demoralized? If that hasn't happened already?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Come here to gloat, huh. I beat him! I beat the Titan! Well, hardly matters. He's still alive, you realize, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wasn't coming here to gloat, and yes, I realize. But this is still a step. If I could vanquish Chronos once, I can do it again. And one of these nights... he's not going to come back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. 'Cause one of these nights, I'll get my hands on him myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Had one of my dreams, Nem. Your mother was there, and my father. He showed concern for her, she... was under the Titan's spell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Mother Nyx made a mistake entrusting Hades with so much of the Underworld. This realm was never his by right, not till the Fates decided otherwise. Mother worked hard. They were probably just trying to send her help.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're saying the Fates granted my father authority over the realm of the dead just to give Nyx a hand with all her responsibilities?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis, about the dream I had... Chronos, he... demanded that my father tell him where the Fates reside.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Smart. Mother Nyx never would have told him. The Fates may be my sisters but even I have no idea where they are. Long as they're hidden, no one can mess with their designs.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't sound especially concerned that Chronos apparently found the three weavers of destiny and may be forcing their plans for the future.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Really? I ever give you the impression I had any hope this would end well?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How could he have found them? Wouldn't the Fates see the threat coming?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: They're always weaving. That means even they know only so much in advance. Chronos is Time. Time moves faster than expected.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis, about the dream I had... Chronos, he... demanded that my father tell him where the Fates reside.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Smart. Mother Nyx never would have told him. The Fates may be my sisters but even I have no idea where they are. Long as they're hidden, no one can mess with their designs.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't sound especially concerned that Chronos somehow found the three weavers of destiny and cast them into exile. And can no longer find them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Really? I ever give you the impression I had any hope this would end well?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How did he do it...? Wouldn't the Fates see the threat coming?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: They're always weaving. That means even they know only so much in advance. Chronos is Time. Time moves faster than expected.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got careless. All because of a little wager. You want to show me up that bad?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I could ask you the same thing. What are you doing back already? You couldn't have gone much farther.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You passed her little test that time. Must have felt so good beating her ass to the ground. Don't get to spar with her, so I can only imagine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We're not just sparring. You don't know our craft.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Eris is back. What did you do? Did you summon her? I saw you talking.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're not remotely pleased to see your sister again? I don't have any more control over her than you do. Headmistress still is giving her the silent treatment, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Ideally, Eris should be somewhere very far from here. Good thing this place is impossible to find, otherwise I'd half expect she would have led Chronos straight to us by now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You truly think a daughter of Nyx would betray us? I know Eris is a different sort, but still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father is a son of Chronos. How'd they get on?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eris stands against us. Just as I neared my destination on the surface, she was there. Let the Adamant Rail do much of the talking.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Imagine my surprise. So what to do? Can't kick her out, since all children of Nyx are welcome here, whosever bright idea that was. Maybe give her some more second chances?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Isn't there something you can do to talk some sense into Eris? Get her out of my blasted way to Olympus. Or failing that, pin her in an arm bar or leg lock, perhaps?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Won't waste my time with her at all, though the arm bar or leg lock do have a certain appeal. Not seen her flying through the Underworld lately, have you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not other than these Crossroads. Though what's your point, that I should stay out of her way as well as focus solely on Chronos below? It's not so simple for me anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Didn't say it was. But if you don't want to deal with Eris, you could always stick to your main objective. Or just leave it to me, like you should have in the first place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Showed Eris didn't you. Could hear her crying practically from here. Been far too long since last I kicked her ass myself. At least somebody's picked up the slack.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps all of those savage fights between you two contributed to her disagreeable nature. Or yours, for that matter. Unless you're certain neither one of you has changed a bit, in which case, what was all the fighting even for?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You've met her. She always liked picking fights. And you've met me. I've never liked backing down from them. As for you, go learn to take a compliment next time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What's with you and Eris, anyway? Thought you'd have the sense to stay away from her, least when you have a choice.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I may lack proper judgment when it comes to choosing comrades, Nemesis. She and I have little in common, but... she makes it difficult to be completely rational.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, you'll get what's coming to you when it comes to her. Or me, for that matter but in a different way. Maybe that's the appeal. Anyway, just don't go bringing her around.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis, for all the time you used to spend on the surface... surely there was something there that made it worth your while?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, there was. Mortals like to reach beyond their means, thinking they're just like us. I'd sniff them out, and put them back in their place.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Put them back in their place... by punishing them in some inventive fashion, I suppose?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: That's right. We all should get what we deserve, no more, no less.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But don't mortals already have ways to ensure justice is served, like my father did?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey. You miss being up there, don't you? Among the mortals and all that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, mortals have a word for deliberate cruelty or callous negligence: They call it evil. I could always catch wind of it. I'd go to the source. And whoever it was, I'd make them pay.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But now you're stuck here, and the evil goes unchecked...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Another failed attempt. About ready to give in? You don't have the fire. This isn't personal enough for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Isn't personal? You don't see my mother around, either, do you, Nem? Nor my father, nor my brother. Chronos took them all!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I lost more than my mother, too. And I knew mine. What's yours to you? All you ever did was fall out of her womb.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What you said to me before, about my mother. That was very cruel.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hm. Fair enough. Can't kill each other here, but we can use our words. If we were fighting for real I wouldn't have held back, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: How were they, anyway? Your family. Persephone the Queen, Prince Zagreus, whoever else. He doesn't want this blade back yet, does he?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He hasn't mentioned it, he's... I don't know, I like him quite a bit. Perhaps since I haven't been around him for aeons as with siblings from certain other families I've met. And my mother and father, they're... all that I imagined, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Right. Probably because your imagination's filling in a lot of blanks. But, least you found them, and it's off to a good start. Not to be taken for granted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: They're not going to help you, you know. The Fates, I mean. They don't take pity on whoever asks. If anything, you'll make things even worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Maybe you just don't give your sisters enough credit. If they once aided my brother, then there has to be a chance they'll aid us. They have a stake in this, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What, you mean their family? They couldn't care less. Too busy messing with everybody's lives, making it look like an accident.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Back on your feet again there, Princess? All's well in the Crossroads, nothing to report.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's fortunate we have you vigilantly keeping watch here, Nemesis...! Oh come on, drop the act. What happened, when did you return?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know what you're talking about, and it's not your business anyway. Fates help you on your task.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not quite as easy as you imagined, is it? To think that the embodiment of Retribution could be so arrogant.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Does Headmistress know yet of your little excursions? Not much escapes her notice, last I checked.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Strongly advise you mind your own responsibilities. Hecate doesn't control me, and you should show a little gratitude.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For what, endangering us all? I didn't ask for your assistance, Nemesis.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The first few times I saw you out there, I figured you were just venting your anger. But you keep heading out night after night. You're determined, but you're going to get caught.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not if no one says anything. Besides, Hecate likely already knows. She isn't dense. Just letting me learn whatever she thinks I stand to learn the hard way. Works out for both of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fine. We're both learning a lot I think. But I don't intend to let up on any of my responsibilities, just because you're trying to achieve what I've been tasked to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nem. You care so much for mortal justice. Where's your passion for ours? Chronos took everything from us! You said we all should get what we deserve, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Khh. Well, that's the thing. If you knew anything about mortals, they had it better once. When Chronos ruled. Called it the Golden Age.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How splendid that must have been for them! If only Chronos could have been so generous with his sons and foster-children. You think him being somewhat nice to some mortals makes up for what he did?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No. Look. What if... what if this is what we deserve?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We deserve better than this, Nemesis. Living in shadows. Denied our families. Don't you want justice for them?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So just how much trouble are you in for mouthing off to Headmistress, Nem? That was utterly disrespectful, even for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh! Well, looks like I've got a lot more guard duty ahead of me. But it was worth it. Help balance out you sucking up to her all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It figures you have no ability to distinguish between basic courtesy, and what you call sucking up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Was it always like this, between you and Headmistress? I used to think it was me you hated most of all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You've always been naive, Princess. As for Hecate, I liked her better before she decided this right here was the best use of my talents.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She made that decision long ago. So you're going to resent her forever? Or are you going to do something about it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I am doing something about it! I'm honoring my vow to Mother Nyx. Else I'd be long gone, much to your disappointment, I'm sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Do you know Headmistress is gone? Chronos took her right before my eyes! If you were on watch like you ought have been, she... augh!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you talking about, Chronos took her. Saw her just the other night. Though, where is she...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I just said! She's probably there in the House somewhere with the rest, but I... I've not been able to locate her yet. How could this happen, what could we have done?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You're serious. Wait, why didn't you do something about it if you saw the whole thing? I wasn't even there!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was too late! What's your excuse? You could have at least identified the possibility of such a threat!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not even all-seeing Selene saw it coming, how could I? Although... keep this quiet. Get her back, or I will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Been talking to Hypnos again? He can't hear you, you know. Kind of what happens when somebody's fast asleep.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sleep is not as simple as you make it out to be. I think he can sense my words and intentions.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sensing words and intentions, yeah... sounds exactly like the Hypnos I knew.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't believe it! You finally woke Hypnos up, and I slept through the whole thing? He everything you dreamed of?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, erm... his manner wasn't what I expected, and he was not especially appreciative. Although I too am prone to moodiness after sleeping in. Then, he left in a hurry...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Tough break I guess! Always thought he really was your type. Well if you do end up working in your father's House, you'll meet again. For now, least we don't have to listen to him snoring every single night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Getting some use out of that Evil Eye. Anything or anyone out there force you to call it a night early, next night you go do the same to them. Old tradition, but it works.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Appreciate the gift. The Eye has certainly been instrumental in some cases where I got the better of my foes. Though whether I'm delivering retribution or merely taking vengeance, I'm not always sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sorting out each act of vengeance does take a while. But don't sweat the technicalities too much. You're clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Wondered why Hecate took off all of a sudden. Was just to get in your way! Send you crying home. Guess she doesn't want you to succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She expects me to be prepared for whatever lies ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't believe sucking up to the Fates actually worked. That big scroll over there... that's really from them?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Personally delivered by Lord Moros, no less. He seemed a little pressed for time, so we didn't get to talk much. Are the two of you close?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: We're children of Nyx; none of us are close. But we crossed paths. When certain mortals get what they deserve, it involves us both.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Never thought I'd see Moros here of all places. But then again, it only makes sense Doom would come to the Crossroads sooner or later.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You make it sound like it's a bad thing. All Nyx's kin are welcome here. Headmistress gave her word.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Makes sense you and Doom would get along. First I figured he'd put you off like everybody else, but you both like following orders so much. Means you have a lot in common.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't believe we've had a single conversation about how much we love following orders, though we do get on, I think. It doesn't trouble you, does it? You'd tell me if it did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd tell you if it did. Moros never was a happy sort, not that any of us really are; but when he's better off, it probably bodes well for everyone else. Given his connections and all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why all the rancor toward Narcissus, by the way? I see how he could be a little irritating, but he doesn't seem all that bad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Should have seen what he did to this poor Nymph. Absolutely broke her heart. Didn't even notice, or care.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Didn't take you for the sentimental type. I thought you concerned yourself with worse than lovers' quarrels.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Narcissus cared only for himself, not for anyone around him. Had to make an example of him. He wound up madly in love. With his own reflection. Till the day he died.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So now he's a Shade in the Underworld. And still staring longingly into his own eyes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you're the witch, not me. You know how it is with curses.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, about Narcissus. I won't undo the curse you put on him, even if I could. He seems to be rather content, and besides... I can tell you truly believe he deserved it. Only problem is, that curse did nothing to aid Echo in her plight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Echo... that was the Nymph who put Narcissus on my list of things to do. You're telling me getting divine retribution with no strings attached wasn't enough to put her mind at peace?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Correct. You evened the score between them, though you didn't set things right for either one. They had to set things right for themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: With your help and encouragement, I guess? Eh, same difference to me long as neither one ends up much better off. Sounds like you lectured both of them, so I'm sure it's close.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So you don't care how they end up, as long as they're even.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: That Nectar you've been passing around? Don't see why you bother. Not everybody deserves it, anyhow.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Deserves? And I suppose you think you're fit to be the judge of that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. Who do you think I am? If everybody just magickally got what they deserved, I'd have no reason to exist.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I didn't mean to make you uncomfortable, with the Nectar from before. It's just, I've always looked up to you, and... I'm doing the best I can.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't sweat it. Had myself a little drink the other night, and it didn't poison me as far as I know. So we're clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Has anyone ever told you you look a bit like your mother, Nemesis? Having met her I can see the resemblance. In fact, when I first saw her, I... well, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No, finish that thought. When you first saw her, you what?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Erm, when I first saw her, I... look, I was taken aback by her appearance! In a positive way. She was like if you wore an elegant gown, and gave your brow muscles a rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Was your idea of a compliment. Nobody wants to look like their mother. Don't look anything like mine anyway. Except maybe the hair.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Demeter said I look just like my mother, which I thought was very kind of her! Queen Persephone is striking.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Ran into Nyx last night. So whenever she's had her fill of being around Hades and Persephone, she goes off to Chaos for a little quiet time. I can see the appeal.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She's not doing it to get away from my parents! With all that the Underworld's gone through, don't you think Night and Chaos might have a lot to discuss?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure. Trying to imagine what they even talk about. It continues to be very dark here, Chaos. Yes, it does, Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How has it been having your mother back? I'm still growing accustomed to the part where my own birthmother is in my life, even if I may not see her every night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Doubt Mother Nyx will be coming around much either. Never did. But she was never really gone. Night's been around since before Time. Nobody messes with her.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Speaking of that, sounds like you have free rein to dole out Retribution once again, not that you weren't doling it out before. No need to keep pursuing Chronos now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Next time you got something to say about me, you come say it to my face. Know you witches would much rather sneak around, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What are you even talking about? I think you're being accusatory and abrasive. There! I said that to your face.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus tried giving me one of his talks. Telling me you didn't go complaining to him?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He was concerned with how we're getting on. You're not exactly being subtle, and he's quite perceptive in case you hadn't noticed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got yet another Titan up against us, huh? Prometheus! One of the smart ones, so they say. And now, an enemy. Could have been on our side had your family not... you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, the whole chain-him-to-a-mountain-so-an-eagle-could- eat-his-regenerating-liver-for-aeons thing. Not one of the proudest moments on Olympus, I have to imagine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Come ask me whether he deserves his vengeance if ever you want to feel any worse about that whole mess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Let me guess. The Fates have nothing but a bunch of busywork laid out for you. No grand revelations, just odd jobs.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know better than to question their designs. A minor prophecy fulfilled may result in a greater one coming to pass.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When we were together out there, it seems almost like a dream. Especially since it was rather unlike either one of us to have set all our responsibilities aside that way...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You're saying you regret it, fine; I don't. Not like we've made a habit of it, and besides... got you out of my head, just as I wanted.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You did? That isn't how it worked for me at all. I've only thought about you more since then. And I do not regret it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No? Well, that's Retribution for you. Some win, some lose, nobody gets it all. Though maybe over time, we can... even things out. Shouldn't be one-sided between us.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When we were together out there, Nem... I wanted to make sure you weren't angry with me. No more than usual at least. You said I was distracting you and that's not what I want.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I know, it's fine, we're good. Unless you keep making too big a deal. I think we get each other now, and we both like it when we're sure. Anyway, be seeing you out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: When did it start. You couldn't even stand looking at me. Couldn't stand your scrawny ass either. Then something happened. Trying to pinpoint it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My feelings for you gradually strengthened over time. The anger you brought out in me... I started to like it. I just never thought you would reciprocate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Took you for a showoff. Never realized how strong you are, and not just physically. And you don't overdo it anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know I can read you sometimes, Nem, and something's wrong, so what is it? If you don't mind telling me...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, it's... look, I've not been close with anyone for very long. Just how it goes. So, been wondering where things are going with us, and not liking where it leads.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You truly think we're on some fixed path? The Fates may be your sisters but you needn't all be caught up in self-fulfilling prophecies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No, but... problem I'm having is I'm starting to like being around you more than not. If it keeps on that way, I'll be...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I know. Too much of a good thing. But, must we concern ourselves about the future constantly? We could just appreciate being together when we can. No pressure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...No pressure, huh... maybe you're right. Could always find a way to balance it out without messing it up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...How long has it been since you've visited the surface?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Too long. Cooped up here now in case you hadn't noticed. But, it's a lost cause anyway. Why do you ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hermes requested that I pay a visit when I can. Wanted to know what to expect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: It's like I said. The dead are out and about up there thanks to our Titan friend. You'll feel right at home.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...How long has it been since you've visited the surface?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Too long. Cooped up here now in case you hadn't noticed. But, it's a lost cause anyway. Why do you ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hermes requested that I pay a visit when I can. Figured I should ask if you had any notes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: It's like I said. The dead are out and about up there thanks to our Titan friend. You'll feel right at home.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You really went up there. They could have called for somebody with experience, but I guess I don't deserve it as much as I thought.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm needed there as part of my task. Though, they must miss you on the surface. So much suffering...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Justice doesn't matter anymore. The mortals had it coming. Maybe we all did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You keep heading up when you ought to be heading down, toward your objective. Forget about the mountain. Not your fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My family on Olympus is under assault by the legions of Chronos, and it's not my fight?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No. He's down below, all comfy on your father's throne. And you don't have anything to worry about besides him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why don't I ever see you on the surface, Nem? Surely we have foes deserving your attention there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Death to Chronos is the only task. Won't get caught up in the details like you. Each night you spend up in the mountains, the Titan secures his foothold below.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't see how Typhon's reemergence complicates matters? He's not exactly the sort of problem one can ignore for long.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How fare you down in Tartarus now that you've found a filthy tunnel entrance in? Your brute-force efforts to bring death to Chronos bearing any fruit?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No. Still haven't found a way into the House. Been running into lots of Satyrs, though. Thinning the herd, as they say.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well at least you're helping carve a path for me! Though, that's probably the last thing you want. You won't stop until you take the Titan down yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't plan on stopping after or before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: We're getting nowhere. I should be out there fighting! Not standing watch over a place nobody comes to uninvited. Damn it...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think the circumstances justify heightened security around here. But if you don't, go take it up with the Headmistress, not me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't deserve this. Any of this. It's unfair. But you're stuck with it. So the only question is, what are you going to do about it now?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You'll soon find out! Though, how can you be so sure I don't deserve what's happening or that it isn't fair?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry, you didn't miss much while you were out. Lot of standing around, chanting prayers for your victory and protection. How'd those work out for you, by the way?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fine, thanks. And your own lack of support is a more significant motivator than you realize.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: How many attempts was that now? Good thing our Titan friend's too busy with your topside relatives to pay you much attention.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sorry, not been keeping count. I'll get there when I get there. Though surely you would have vanquished the Titan Lord on your very first go?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What's with that throne conspicuously near my post. Just a temptation. Never once seen you sit down on the job; you ever seen me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, it's just... I thought perhaps you'd like the option there at least. Nothing in excess, right? And you do have to stand an awful lot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: My rules, not yours. I start sitting down, won't want to get back up. Especially wearing all this. Besides, half the point of guard duty is having to stay standing the whole time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I always thought... if I could just train harder than you, she'd reconsider. And give the task to me. Worked my ass off. But it didn't matter.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I worked, too. And Headmistress always made herself clear about our roles. You can't change your birthright, nor can I change mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why don't I ever see you in the training grounds anymore? You used to let all your aggression out over there, but now it's like you're saving it for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't flatter yourself. Saving it for Chronos. And the training grounds increasingly are custom-fit for you, in case you haven't noticed. From that Altar, to the weapons display.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You're more conceited than Narcissus. Saw those statues of you in the training grounds. You'll have to work a whole lot harder to look like that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, I... I had nothing to do with those. Well, I did earn them, they're the Gifts of the Veil bestowed upon Night's Champion...! Oh, that does sound quite conceited...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't sweat it. They're something all right. Always said you'd never be bigger than me. That's what I get.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: So now Olympus has to deal with Typhon, huh? Wonder if Chronos made a deal with him or just revived and set him loose. Either way, plan like that takes a lot of guts.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You sound rather impressed! I'm certain Chronos would welcome you into his ranks as well if you just tell him how you really feel.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hit a nerve? Fine. Doesn't change the fact our enemy outsmarted us. So, no need to keep wasting your time up top... wait, you're not going to try and fight that thing, are you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You really think I'm going to leave my family on Olympus to face that monster on their own? You don't know me at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: They're saying you got rid of Typhon for good. Don't believe a word of it. What really happened up there?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Got rid of Typhon for good is a concise way to put it. Involved a rather complex incantation called Disintegration of Monstrosity and some other details best left unspoken. But yes, I do believe he's gone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: He's immortal... he can't just be gone, and if he is, why not just do the same to Chronos? Or to anybody who stays on your bad side long enough?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We've something else in store for Chronos. As for everybody else, they haven't crossed me quite as much just yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Must have got a lot of help, knocking Typhon from his perch. Oh, we all heard. You got a dozen family members all up there, some of them strong. So what do they need you for?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They certainly are capable, but they're backed into a corner on Olympus. What matters is our latest battle was victorious, and I did what I could to see it through.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sharing in your success, or just trying to appease me? Main reason you won is that monster had it coming anyway. But, it'll all even out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Really must be making Typhon mad. What's the plan there besides that? Get so caught up in all the glory that you lose sight of our real enemy?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Father of All Monsters represents an urgent threat; you'd know it if you saw him for yourself. If Olympus is laid to ruin, Chronos prevails. We wouldn't be able to stop him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Speak for yourself. The Underworld never needed any of those gods. Chronos wouldn't even be here if not for them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't kill Chronos, can't kill Typhon, so what now? Keep doing this till somebody gives up? Thought we had a better plan.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Titan thinks he took the upper hand by awakening that monster. But we'll turn that setback into our gain. Typhon is immensely powerful; a power we can use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You really think the Titan would just offer up the key to his own undoing like that? Doesn't sound particularly smart.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The plan was always to exploit what he doesn't know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: The hell are you doing talking to me? You get your scrawny ass back out there, and get that Titan! What are you waiting for?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Even Mother Nyx's weapons favor you. Always thought the Nocturnal Arms would wake for me first. Guess the Fates had other ideas.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think the Fates had you covered. You're the bearer of Stygius! Vanquisher of heroes, gods, and Titans.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis, we've been going on like this a while, and I wondered if you'd be up to take a break. Over at the Springs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hm. Come to think, could really use a bath. You, I mean. But I could too, I guess, so... why not.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How long has it been since you last took off the armor?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: A while. Not been much incentive. You saw how much of a pain it is to undo the lacing, and the straps. Feels strange without, like I'm... I... I don't know.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Unburdened? Or more comfortable at least?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh. Good letting off some steam sometimes, I guess. But there's a fine line between rest and complacency.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't know that we're at risk of becoming complacent here, Nem.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: The risk is greater than you think. We're lounging in a bath while who-knows-what is going on out there...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We're not lounging. We'll be better equipped to deal with the who-knows-what out there if our muscles aren't all knotted up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, got to get back to work. Though, this suit does feel a little lighter now...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey Nem, are you available to make another visit to the springs? Take a load off, come back to our senses a bit...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh. Shoulder's been acting up again, could be it helps with that. Don't like having to take the suit off, but that's the trade.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: First time you beat me in a fight? Sparring match, whatever. Got me right in the back of the neck. Took advantage of our height difference. Was a surprise.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd lost to you so often, at some point I figured I could lull you into overconfidence. That even you could be susceptible to such a thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You weren't wrong. Made me so angry. Swore I'd never underestimate my opponent again. Then proceeded to convince myself you'd never beat me at the task. Our task.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So you've a bit of pride. You're not trying to stamp it out completely, right? Just keep it in check. Besides, I'd like to think we're not at odds in what we're trying to achieve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: We're not. But I can only push myself so hard. Seeing you getting stronger, getting closer... gives me the extra push I need.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd no idea. I always thought I got my competitive spirit from you. Anyway... do let me know if you need anything else. Such as lending a hand putting on that suit of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Or taking it off, yeah. Though, can't let myself become too used to this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, enough sitting around. Time for some standing around to balance it out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You never did say no to Bath Salts, Nemesis, so how about it? As long as you feel we've earned at least a short break.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know that we have. Though I've accumulated enough knots in my muscles that it's gotten in the way. So why not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't believe I'm sitting here with you. Couldn't stand the sight of you for the longest time. Wanted to snap you like a twig. Now I got you helping me unstrap my suit.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We're capable of change, aren't we? You must believe that yourself, otherwise what's the use of all the retribution you dole out? It can't be for the sake of punishment entirely.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: The punishment is half of it. But yeah. Important to learn, if not from our own failures then those occurring constantly around us. Still I don't think I've changed at all; you have.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have? I'm still hell-bent on all my responsibilities. Counting the moments before I can get back to what I'm meant to be doing... rather than enjoying being here with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Except we're only here because you worked up the guts to ask. But fine, maybe you haven't changed a lot. Maybe I just like who you are more than I used to for some reason.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I feel much the same. Though, I've admired you since training. It gladdens me to be with you like this... and how we've been fighting on the same side, recently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Always wanted the same thing, just didn't think there was enough to go around. Now, enough talk. Going to get these knots out of my back or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Feels better than before, I'll give you that. Though don't go thinking I'll let up on you out there. We're calling this even.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, erm, I suspect I know the answer, though I don't suppose you want to pay a visit to the springs? Clear our heads?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, uh... Appreciate the invite, but I'm on guard duty right now, probably until Time claims us all. And this suit takes forever to get on or off...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Think there's something wrong with my suit. Got time to take a look?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Choice_NemesisBecomingCloser01

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, let me help you with that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Your suit still seems to be in proper working order.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Better. Should hold me over for a little while.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, now just isn't a good time...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Like it when it's this quiet. Nobody's here but us. We could... I mean...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Choice_NemesisBecomingCloser01

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't need to say anything, Nem...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Guess breaktime's over but good running into you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Important to take breaks from time to time, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm in a bit of a rush tonight is all...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Was just about to go but had a feeling you'd show up. Want to stay a bit?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Choice_NemesisBecomingCloser01

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, I think I do.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...It's nice taking it slow once in a while, isn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Should probably get on with it, though least we took some time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can't right now, sorry...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey if you're not in any hurry, it's been quiet around here, so...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Choice_NemesisBecomingCloser01

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not in any real hurry, no...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Whew. I suppose we should get on with it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hm. Nice place when it's not crawling with things needing to be killed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sorry but this isn't a good time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't have to hurry off. No one's going to notice if you're back a little late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Choice_NemesisBecomingCloser01

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't need much convincing, Nem.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Well as you said, no need to rush.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Both ought to get back to it, but should do this again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd better not. Perhaps some other time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh, got something stuck here in my suit and need to get it out. You mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Choice_NemesisBecomingCloser01

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't mind at all. Let's have ourselves a look.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Feels much more comfortable for now, so thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Should be all better for a while, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Forgive me, but not tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Trade you something for some Gold. Got to keep Charon fed and all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Need some Gold and won't be fair if I just beat it out of you, so how about a trade.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What say we make a little deal if you've got the coin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got something you could use though you're not getting it for free.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can help you go farther than this, but it's not gonna come cheap.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Want to be the best, it's not gonna come cheap.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Make you a deal, but only if you've got the coin for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You're a princess, you must have some coin to spare, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: How much Gold you get out there? Can make it worth your while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Could shake you down for all your Gold, but wouldn't be fair.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Special offer for you, Princess. Pay now, or it's gone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Could use a little extra coin and you could probably use this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Imagine I'm Charon and got just the thing you need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, look who! Also lost here in these Fields I guess? Trade you a map or something if I had one, but you wouldn't have been able to afford it, so I'd use it to get out of here myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I doubt a map would be of any use, as these pathways seem to shift from night to night. I've been able to make use of the Golden Boughs scattered about at least. What about you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: The Golden Boughs. Had no idea those did anything but look all sad. Guess I should have paid better attention when the Headmistress was droning on and on over that Cauldron.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Always used to be Gold in the Underworld, right in its veins. But Chronos turned it into little coins! All over the damn place now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Charon's going to take care of it, soon enough. I give him all the Gold I find. Trade for his wares.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Think I can do the same, if you want to make a deal. Don't worry, any Gold I get from you, Charon gets from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Mortals must be thinking they can get away with anything lately...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Awful busy standing guard as you can see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: So much evil happening out there, unchecked...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Go on. That task of yours isn't going to complete itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Could've been topside right now, bringing down what's rightfully deserved...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not going anywhere, so go on and do your task.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: We all get what we deserve. No more, no less.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Missed all the excitement while you were out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Go knock yourself out. I'll be right here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Excuse me, I'm on duty still, as you can see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Ungh, what did I ever do to deserve this...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Back on the beat again already there, Princess?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Go crack some skulls for me, they have it coming.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can only imagine what you've been up to out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Counting on you not to get too greedy out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Better get your scrawny ass back out there huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Go cry to your Headmistress Hecate. She's right there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: The ways of the Witch of the Crossroads can only take you so far.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sooner or later, everything always comes full circle, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You were topside for a while there, huh...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Gone so long that time, figured you lost your way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: All the way back, just to lick your wounds and go again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought for sure you would have given up by now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: All's quiet in the Crossroads, for the thousandth time...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Just going to keep putting yourself through this, huh.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got the pointless guard duty well under control.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Go on, you've got more important things to do than talk to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Want to take over for me, go right ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Find Chronos, kill Chronos. Go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Death to Chronos, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Ready to give up, tell Hecate I'm still available.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got back not so long ago myself, though didn't do so well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Maybe see you out there again tonight if you haven't had enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Haven't seen you out there in a few nights, so maybe we're due.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sticking to the surface for a while or will I see you out there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Things looking any better on the surface recently or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Took care of things in Tartarus while you were busy topside.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Leave the Underworld to me, and go take care of things up top.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I can handle Chronos again if you want to deal with Typhon instead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll be sticking to the Underworld if you want to head up top instead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Get back out there, and maybe I'll catch up once I take care of a few things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: One word of this to Hecate, and you're meat, got it? Now go on while I take care of things.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is my task, not yours. And I'm disappointed with your dereliction of duty. Are you so desperate to prove you're superior to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, maybe I am. So how about this: Got some Gold on you, right? Those wretches ought to swarm in any moment. Whoever takes more of them gets the other's coin. What do you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Going to take care of Chronos myself if you can't do it. But first things first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Once we're done here, one of us gets richer, and one of us gets poorer.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: How did you get here already? Ungh, anyway. It's your Gold against mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can hardly stand all this water and the singing, but this has to be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Was just about to clear the way, so why don't you pitch in.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Fates must be smiling on you, as I'm about to do all the work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Should be more than enough wretches for the both of us here, any moment now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't need assistance from you here, but how about we make things interesting...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll handle this, and once I'm finished with the work, I'm getting paid.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's see whether the Fates are on your side or mine this time around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: To think I could be standing there on guard duty right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember to be extra reckless since your Gold is on the line.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Could use more coin. How about I take yours, soon as we're finished around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Get set to fight as though there's something you could buy from Charon on the line.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Showed up just as I was about to settle up all by myself. Well you can have your share.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's split the killing down the middle, give or take. As in, I'll take your Gold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't pull your weight, I'll take your Gold soon as I wrap this up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got the Blade of the Underworld. Won't be needing your help here, thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Want a bit of extra Gold, then earn it. Now heads up, they're coming.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: How about a little friendly competition there, Princess? Come on, let's see it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: They say Chronos likes Gold, so he won't mind our little contest here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: So this is where the real magick happens, huh. Let's see what the best of the Titan's armies have to offer.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Guess we're the ones haunting these woods at this point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Split some of these fish freaks with me, or should I just take your gold?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not everybody in the Fields deserves to be here, but right now it can't be helped.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I can take care of this myself but it's more interesting if you get in the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, no fair. How come you get two blades when I only get one?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: That axe looks a little big for you. Just the way you like it, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Using that skull again. Not a real weapon but whatever works.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, it's my brother's scythe. Let's see if you're any better with it than he was.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Charon used to lug that axe around. Must be sucking up to him again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not a proper suit of armor you've got on but it's a start.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you say we make things interesting again, Princess?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure beats standing around in the Crossroads. Especially when I get your Gold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Better move those legs, Princess, else I'm taking all your Gold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got to say these wagers haven't worked out well for me lately.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: All this Gold is gonna go to Charon anyway, so what's it matter who gets more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Stand around and watch me work, or fight. Same difference either way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You think your scrawny ass is going to beat me kill for kill right here and now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Been far too long since I took all your Gold, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not seen you down here in a bit. Been too busy up top?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Practically taste the Fear in the air. Let's make them fear us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You and that Oath making things a lot harder than they need to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: All that pent-up anger. Let it out, then. I feel anything, I'll make it worth your while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Wretches couldn't even hit me. Wasn't fair. How about you make it up to them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at you acting tough. Go on, hit me hard as you can. Free shot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's see how strong you really are. Hit me, and don't hold back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can tell you're angry. Then let it out and show me what you're made of.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at you, all tough and fierce. Show me how strong you really are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: To the victor go the spoils. So come show me what you've got.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: None of these wretches could touch me. But you're stronger than them, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't think you have what it takes, though go on and try to prove me wrong.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Getting stronger, but you'll never be at my level. Go on, try me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You've been getting stronger, but can't always tell by how much. So show me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got here well before you did. Cleaned up, too. Makes you mad, I know. Don't hold back... let's see how strong you really are.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't want to fight you, Nemesis. I don't even want to speak with you right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not asking you to fight me. Asking you to hit me. See if you can even make me budge. It'll be worth your while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Already hit a wall! Knocked your scrawny ass all the way back here. It's insane that she's sending you out on foot, all on your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're fortunate you have even this responsibility, for all your questioning of orders and complaining.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I thought I saw some movement by the fishing pier. A thorough investigation is probably in order, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Either that or rot here standing guard, right? Know what, fine, I'll bite. Doubt anything else will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: To have been born a fish. A life spent mucking about in the River of Lamentation, only to be plucked out by you. What did they do to deserve this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Shouldn't you be the one to know the answer to that? Perhaps it's the will of the Fates that determines how we're born. Then you ensure we get our dues based on the choices that we make.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Be a lot easier if everyone was born on equal footing. But no one in my family thinks life's meant to be fair, other than me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How can life possibly be fair, knowing there are creatures of all sorts, such as those dwelling in these waters?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: We'd have to put a bit more structure into who decides how mortals are born. Or how their souls come back to life. Topic for a different time. Think I saw a bit of movement over there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Gave them a fair shot, didn't we. Real battle of wits back there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Say, would you accompany me to the riverbank a bit? Blow off some steam, or... maybe I could push you in or something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Could be going after perpetrators of unspeakable evil across the surface, but you're asking me to go after some poor fish? Uh, no.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You need all the help you can get, so here! Don't need this anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: So much useless junk lying around. Here, ought to be you cleaning it up, not me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Won't be needing this at all, but you look like you will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Pretend you never saw me, and I didn't make things easier for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Wow, you really showed them, didn't you. And earned a handsome prize.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Best keep your mouth shut about this next time you and Hecate catch up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll see who gets there first. Here, I'll even give you a boost.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You travel lighter than me, so here, why don't we balance out the load.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Was just about to throw this out when you came along...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No way you'll get there first. Even give you a head start.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't really need this but thought I'd hang onto it for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Little treat for you, Princess. Not Nectar but it's something.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Feeling generous so won't be charging you a single coin for this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Never know what to do with these except hand them to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Almost there. This should help you go the rest of the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Missed all the fun. But least you won't have to get your hands dirty this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis, what are you doing here? Who's standing watch at the Crossroads?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: They'll manage, I'm on break. Tell Hecate you saw me, you'll regret it. And, you're welcome here and safe, seeing as I cleared the path for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't need your aid in this, other than the responsibilities you've already been assigned.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Really? Look like you could use all the aid you can get. Here's something for your trouble, then I suggest you move along.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You're late. Already took care of things here, though you'll still get your share, I'm sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you insist on trying to hunt Chronos down, why don't we work at it together, at least?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't be seen with you, and don't need your help, besides. But I get you need mine, so here. Take this, and you never saw me. Got it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Expecting someone else? Taking a moment to get my bearings. Was nice and quiet here till you showed up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You haven't seen a gentle-looking Nymph nearby, have you? She's often here on this bridge.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Might be I scared her off. Got more important things to do than chasing Nymphs. Anyway, go on. The spirits in this place won't like us making nice.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis? Maybe we could start again. Here, I got you something...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't need your charity, Princess, or temptations while I'm on duty or off. You're doing this because you want something from me anyway, so here! Knock yourself out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You trying to get me in trouble with Hecate or something? Why don't you take your bottle before I smash it, and get your scrawny ass out of here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wasn't trying to get you in trouble, Nem, I was just... ungh. You know what, forget it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, for me? Can't find another way to test one of your poisons, I guess. You witches aren't as subtle as you think.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's exactly what someone who was unknowingly poisoned by a witch might say, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh no, not this again. Look, I forgave you for last time. Didn't mean it as an invitation to get me more of this stuff. Don't like owing anybody any favors.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Just take it and enjoy it, Nem. Besides, I heard you were looking for some more of our witches' poison. Nobody seems to appreciate it quite like you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Why do you keep doing this? You won, OK? You beat me. Before you even started. Last thing I need now is your sympathy.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis... I don't know what I'm supposed to do. We're surrounded by enemies out there. Do we really need more?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You're not my enemy, Melinoë. I just... Just go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Suggest you quit it with these Nectars soon. Can't have too much of a good thing. Should know that about me by now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sure, you can't have too much of a good thing anymore than anybody else. Though, what would happen if you did? You'd need to punish yourself?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. And don't you dare act like you wouldn't do the same. One thing I'll grant Hecate is she does value discipline. Sometimes we have to discipline ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know you dislike excesses of any kind, though I don't think one more Ambrosia bottle's going to tip the scales for you, is it, Nem?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: It might. Won't go to the taverna to find out, so why don't you take it back. Not really the time for luxuries.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How about you hold onto it until whenever you feel it's deserved. If you're not indulging, you don't need to be concerned about it, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Want to waste something like this, fine. But it's just going in a stash, and probably will never see what little light we get. Long as you're good with that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
