dialogueLines.text: Thought I'd bring you something extra for your never-to-be-used stash, Nemesis. Because you might deserve it at some point, you never know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You never know. What makes you even think I might deserve something like this? Not exactly been at the top of my field lately.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well I am not the arbiter of such matters of course, but I think you do deserve it. You must be harder on yourself than anyone. If only you could see yourself as I do sometime.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know what that's supposed to mean. We were going for the same result, and both pushed hard. So what. But anyway, into the stash this goes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Ambrosia. Look, you should know my never-to-be-used stash is just about full. Wouldn't want for it to overflow.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You could always invite me to the taverna where we might cut into that stash of yours a bit. Why hoard it forever? Besides the reasons that you previously said.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not going to invite you anywhere long as you keep this up. And I don't appreciate you making light of the subject of avoiding excess. My area of expertise. Not yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm sorry, Nemesis, I wasn't aiming to be rude; words I cannot entirely believe I'm saying, much less feeling, since we used to trade barbs all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Things sure were nice and simple then, weren't they. But now we're stuck in whatever pattern this is, till we break free, and we're doing that now. No more Ambrosia. Ever.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Here, Nemesis! To your willful disobedience. May those instincts of yours never lead you astray.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Really think this is the perfect time for us to just kick back and have a few laughs over a nice bottle? Like I got nothing better to do right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: All these hand-outs from your relatives... surely you don't need them all?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got something here you need, but it'll cost you some of your false power from Olympus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Give me some of your borrowed strength, and you'll get some of mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't need Olympus handing everything to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Want what I've got, got to give something up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't have Olympus helping me, only fair neither should you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: The mountain gods don't offer me their aid, though what about you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Olympus isn't gonna care if you give up one little blessing, will they?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What would it take for you to give up some of your Olympian muscle?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Always depending on your relatives for help. How about you give it up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I get a little boost from Artemis, but you? Just about all Olympus backs you up, and even still, it's not enough. Some help they are.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Direct your criticism to the gods themselves. Now what is it you want with me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Want a little taste of all that power for myself. Come on, I'll trade you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Doesn't all that heavy armor slow you down? Can't be too comfortable, especially around the waist.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't be too comfortable is right. Besides, not everybody wants to run half-naked into battle.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You really ought to try running half-naked into battle. You might like it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Ran into Artemis back there. Between her and now me, we must have done half the heavy lifting for you tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Must be what I deserve, though, right? And don't worry, there should be plenty of foes ahead whom I shall have to deal with on my own. How is the goddess of the hunt this eve?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Seems well enough, we had a decent chat. Was beginning to think she's been avoiding me a while, though I can't imagine why she'd want to do a thing like that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Across the Fields of Mourning, where the path descends to Tartarus... the monstrous creature there... do you know what it is?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: It's a problem. Can't always find my way to it, and even when I do, that thing forces me back. But don't worry; got my eye on it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You must have thought you would have taken Chronos down by now. Have you even seen him? Gotten anywhere near my father's House?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep hitting snags along the way. The pathways down to Tartarus have changed a lot. Especially once you get to the Fields.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have a sense for where we need to go. I'd suggest we proceed together had Headmistress not strictly forbidden it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not happening, no matter what she says. Doing this alone. Better odds that way anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Quit stalling. Sooner you fail, sooner I'll get to show you how it's done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Say, how do you get past Headmistress on your way down? She's always waiting for me at the edge of Erebus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Just answered your own question. She's waiting for you, not me. I'm not the one still being tested and trained. And I'm good at avoiding her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, you brought the Evil Eye I gave to you, how sweet. Make your enemies regret messing with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm certain our foes will get what's coming. This Eye, though; it almost looks alive. Not using it to spy on me, are you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Nah. See enough of you as it is. Now get out of here before that Eye does anything to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Damn it! How many times you going to take my Gold? Rules of this wager might be too much in your favor after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You set the rules of this wager yourself. You'd never go easy on me, would you, Nem? Though you can't bring yourself to make things overly unfair for me, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Beginning to think it might be a weakness of mine. Well, go buy yourself something nice, Princess. Don't need to hear more metal clinking around every step I take anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All of this water must be quite a nuisance in your suit. Not found another pathway down, have you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Only other path is through Elysium, and that's not happening for you or me. What better way for Chronos to show he's in charge than to seal off the Underworld for good?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Good thing he missed a spot. Although he realizes now that we're using this path. That I am using it, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Thanks for the tip! No wonder the fish are biting. Anyway, I'm off. This suit isn't supposed to rust, but just in case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought for sure when first you saw me out here, you'd go telling Hecate soon as you could. But you didn't.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's fortunate for you Headmistress doesn't ask me to make reports. You're probably drawing some of our enemy's attention off of me, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Very least I could do. Wasn't going to back down no matter what you did, but this way... can stay focused. On the task at hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Who needs to sip Ambrosia in the Crossroads when we could be out here doing this together, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Vanquishing our foes with impunity is your idea of a good time? Although that's not to say I don't take satisfaction in a job well done either. Wait what did you mean by together?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh shut up. We used to spar, this is the closest thing. Don't know anybody else who can keep up, let alone get ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A healthy competitive rivalry is all we have between us then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I said shut up. What else do you want it to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I broke a sweat? I broke a sweat. That never happens, those were weaklings, I... oh damn...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What's the matter, Nem, are you all right? Our foes may have resorted to using poisons and such, if I could look at any superficial wounds...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't touch me! It's not that. Now get on with it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Have you been able to get past Scylla and her Siren friends? Just once I'd like to pass through and not have to hear their song...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: They know better than to mess with me. Wait, you've been fighting them? How'd you make enemies with a musical act?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I... can't entirely be sure. But they've been very hostile to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Been working with Selene, huh? Never thought her moon-magick was worth much, not from all the way up there. My blade... whenever I need it, I can use it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Whatever works, right? I'm not here to try and persuade you about magick, Nemesis. You already made up your mind. Though, you and Selene don't get on too well, do you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not even one of Nyx's own and she still favored her. Maybe because she was always a suck-up like you are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Selene really helped you out in all of this, huh. Helped us out, I mean. Thought she'd just quietly look down from on high but she really got in there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't know what we would have done if not for her. Besides lending me her strength night after night, she was instrumental in coordinating with our contacts on the surface.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: We daughters of Nyx don't always treat each other very well. Fighting for Mother's affection, or whatever's going through our heads. Anyway... I misjudged her.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think you should tell her, not me. She's probably listening.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you even get out of making things harder on yourself like this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Funny question coming from someone who squeezes into the most uncomfortable suit of armor yet devised. Enacting the Testaments of Night is worth a bit of added effort.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You're just trying to feed her weapons, make them and you stronger. Don't get greedy. Never ends well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, we made it. You realize when you mess with the Oath you're not just making things worse for you, but for me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh I don't know about worse. We serve the realm. It was never going to be easy. I'm surprised you'd want it to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What I want, Princess, is for it be just right. So I can do what needs to be done without failing more than I have to.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's not a failure if you learn from it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: First wager in a while. Been busy up top, breathing surface air? Thought maybe you came to your senses and left things to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I'm not finished here by any means. And evidently neither are you. Still at it like before? Quite difficult, I know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me something, and don't you dare mock me or you'll regret it: How do I... get into Tartarus. Managed to make it through the Fields, then hit a dead end. Kept hitting it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Tartarus is sealed off. The only reason I'm able to get in is because not even Chronos can take away my birthright and divine authority over the place. Although if not for Headmistress, I wouldn't have known how to use it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: No help at all, forget I even asked. Got to be some other way inside, and if not, just have to make one I guess. For now, enjoy the lack of competition while you can.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Any progress locating a means to enter Tartarus? I'd transfer you my birthright if I could under the circumstances, though I'm reasonably certain that's not possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got my own birthright, thanks. And yeah, been making progress. Three-headed monster near the maintenance access point to Tartarus... it digs up through the ground.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You plan to get into the lowest reaches of the Underworld through the hell-hole that beast leaves behind? You know that's Cerberus, my father's dog, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: All the more reason that tunnel's going to lead me right where I need to be, one of these nights. Mark my words.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Told you I'd make it all this way. Nice place your father's got. Though I guess Chronos since refurbished everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Congratulations on infiltrating the lowest reaches, Nemesis. And if you've been drawing out some of our enemy's forces, all the better; plenty to go around. Reached Chronos yet?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got to what has to be the House of Hades, but you know what? They wouldn't let me in. Tried knocking, tried smashing down the door, no good. So Charon took me back.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You got Charon to take you on his boat? He's never once permitted me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not quite as comfortable as being in the springs, is it, Princess? And can be solitary work at that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It needn't be, but you refused back when I suggested we could go together through these depths. Now having second thoughts?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Been having thoughts, but of a different kind. You got into my head. I want you out. You always tried distracting me back when we sparred and now, you made it worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What are you talking about, what did I do...? ...Oh. By having thoughts you mean... feelings. So what now...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know yet, but... well we could stay here a bit, till enough is enough. Since it's quiet. Not binding you, just... too much solitude lately, and want to even it all out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Choice_NemesisBecomingCloser01

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Even it all out... well if we're to make ourselves comfortable and stay quiet, then your armor has to go. Why don't I lend a hand with that, and then we'll go from there?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Well your armor's definitely harder to put back on, but we got there. You all right, Nem? The distraction gone away at least...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Think so, and yeah, I'm better now. I'm good. What about you, are you... I mean...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm also good! This was a thrill...! We ought be on assignment, not alleviating solitude, but... you'd know better than anyone whether this was too much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Was just enough for now. Should get going, though could always do this again should the need arise.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm much like you in not desiring too much of a good thing. I'm supposed to be on task, you're supposed to be on duty; I just don't think my mind would be at ease. Tell me you understand?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I do. Forget I asked. Least we got to talk like this. Should get going, but... be seeing you around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Surprised you didn't bail out with that return trick of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't know anything about me. What are you thinking, being this far from your post, in violation of Headmistress Hecate's direct orders?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm thinking you don't have what it takes to take the Titan down. I do. Now, get out of here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Knew you weren't finished, and you know what? Neither am I. Whatever it is you're doing now, I'm not being left out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sure! Just have a chat with Headmistress or Grandfather Chronos and they'll fill you in on everything we're up to now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Right. Meantime, anybody asks, I'm just out to stretch my legs and keep my sword arm in top form.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard you found the Three Fates. Don't believe that's how it happened. Probably weren't even looking. They found you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That is a distinct possibility. Perhaps when Chronos thought he sent them into exile, they merely played along. And now decided to check in after the troubles with him passed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure sounds like something they'd do. But no use speculating, right? And what now, we're all back to having no will of our own? Maybe we never did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps they don't want us to be certain either way. Nor do they seem to desire all the credit for the actions we take.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Guess we'd better act like we have everything under control.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Finally back from your father's House. Was Hecate all right? After the Titan got her, I... ought to have taken guard duty more seriously, and... won't soon forgive myself, is all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress would only want for you to learn from it. I half-wonder if she let herself be taken just to get closer to our mark. Perhaps go speak with her...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. And what of Chronos, heard he isn't really dead and gone. How come everybody's cheering for you then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's dead and gone in the sense that we have neutralized the threat he posed. I now shall have to grow accustomed to having him as a grandfather, rather than a hated foe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't see why he can't be both. This was never part of the task, and not like you to stray from the objective. We sure this supposedly-neutralized Titan of Time is to be trusted?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress sealed his strongest powers just in case. At any rate... we did it, Nem!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Then how come you look like you're headed out again? Heard there's still more Time-related business to sort out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: So you're still fighting Chronos, and you're still fighting Typhon... though, not just a matter of where, but a matter of when. Something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Something like that. Our Chronos, who had let's say a spiritual awakening of sorts, is seeing to it that time flows freely forth, to use his expression. We wish to prevent even the possibility of what happened before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I don't get it. What happened before happened already. Time is a simple concept; keeps everything moving forward for everybody at the same exact rate.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Still sticking to the Underworld even with the task complete? There must be lots of catching up to do on the surface.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hecate says conditions have to be just right for whatever it is you're trying to do now. Wouldn't want to throw anything off.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Appreciate the help. It's just I thought you missed being up there, and mortalkind must miss you, too. A lot of undeservedness to be addressed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Keenly aware, thanks. I'll get back to it after the Underworld's in a better spot. Besides, I think your family's paying close attention to what's going on up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard you snuffed another Typhon out last night. Well done, Princess. Things are handled in the Underworld, too, when it comes to time flowing freely forth and all that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Things are handled in the Underworld, how? Wait, you've been speaking with Grandfather Chronos, haven't you? Which means... no...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Your grandfather did what you never could, and got me in the House of Hades. A remnant one of them, containing the old Chronos we were after all along. And I got him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You got him? Vanquished Chronos on your very first attempt? Impossible... even if you did prevail you'd most likely end up trapped for all eternity in a temporal rift...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You really did eliminate one of the Chronos remnants, Grandfather confirmed. That's... amazing, Nemesis. So then, if I focus my efforts on the Typhon remnants on Olympus...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I can deal with the Chronos remnants in the Underworld. Was planning on keeping that up no matter what. Hecate even gave me the all-clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Just, unbelievable. Though I still do reserve the right to deal with Chronos remnants on my own. If nothing else, that route gives me an opportunity to see my family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Knock yourself out. I get the impression there are more than enough of those potential Chronos remnants out there for the both of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Brutal night. Had to work my ass off getting to this point. Everything's hitting back hard. This better not be your doing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Technically it's the Oath of the Unseen's doing rather than mine. What's the matter, don't you want your mother's wishes to be fulfilled?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: So it was you, messing with the Oath. You're insane, you know that? Now here they come. You better be ready.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Scary out there, isn't it, Princess? Didn't take my advice about the Oath, huh. Decided more Fear was for the best.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I didn't think the Fear would get to you, Nemesis! Tell me, what are you so afraid of? You can handle anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: I can handle a lot. But I'm not insane enough to think I can handle anything. Whatever, no time to chat, they're coming.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, you ready to break a sweat again, Nem?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't insult me else I'll take more than your Gold after this. Now come on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at all this business you drummed up, Charon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't you see my brother and I are in the middle of something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Leave me alone, trying to make a trade.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Get in line. I was here first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Talk later. Counting through my Gold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Last chance to stock up for a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Charon keeps his mouth shut and you better do the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Go about your business like I'm not even here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Spending some quality time with my brother here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll get more out of Charon here than me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Urgh, got to hit something sometimes. You know how it is. Could make it worth your while...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Blood's boiling still... got to get it out of my system. Just one clean hit, that's all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Spilled some blood getting my hands on this. Spill some of yours if you want it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You're tough. Can take a hit, can't you. Worth it for both of us...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You'd have taken more than one hit getting through this mess...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Took a hard hit getting here, how about you take one too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Want something from me, you'll pay with blood.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Want to see this through, better be ready to bleed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Doubt you could even handle just a single hit from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Thinking if you can't take a hit from me, you don't have a chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You could always take a hit, Princess. So how about now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Want to know just how hard I got hit cleaning this place?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: These wretches were nothing. Back when we used to spar... almost never got a clean hit in on you. You'd always slip out of the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What choice did I have? One clean hit from you was more than I could take back then. But I've been practicing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You're tougher, yeah. Though by how much? How about I hit you right now, and if you can take it, you'll get this nice reward I found. Deal?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You following me or something? Not used to running into you this many times per night. Find your own path.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Find my own path? You mean besides the one our Headmistress and Master Tactician endlessly toiled to refine to maximize my chances of success?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Just stay out of my way. But first, how about you let me take a good clean swing at you, since you've been mouthing off?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I know there's an extraordinary likelihood you're going to say no, but... I've an extra-special poison bottle here, and thought maybe we could split it at the taverna, you and I?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: So damn polite! Well, it's less excessive if we split the bottle, I guess... ungh, you know what? Whatever, sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Come on, say it: Got your ass kicked. By me. Think that was when I first thought I had a chance. That Hecate would pick me. If I could take you just like that a few more times.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're a monster, Nem! I practically started to fear having to spar with you. But, the best way to get stronger is to train against a stronger opponent.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Maybe that's why I've been stagnating. We don't get to fight like we used to. Makes me sick even thinking about it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait... you stand around thinking about how we used to spar? Should I be flattered or afraid?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Finish your half of the damn poison already! Don't have all night to sit around and stroke your ego. I swear, mortals take after our worst qualities.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wasn't being serious. Though, maybe I'm more self-centered than I realize. I am part-mortal on my mother's side...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Makes you different, I will give you that. Now come on, I got to get back. Else Hecate might catch us making nice and then neither one of us will hear the end of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Can feel the poison starting to kick in. You'd better get your ass out of here before I keel over.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought I told you no Ambrosia anymore. Don't ask again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Charon, get it through your skull, this is not what I ordered. You gonna give me a refund or not?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Nrrnnn, rrnnngghhhhh...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You even listening to a word I'm saying? Ungh, why do I bother.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Which one of us brought you more Gold? You can tell me. Give me a sign. Nod twice if it's me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrnnnngghh, grrrr...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: That looked like one nod... or maybe it was two...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...No. One last thing, Eris. Hecate might have promised Mother she'd look after you, but I did no such thing. So get out of here before I get upset.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh please, Nemmie, you're already upset! And I'll come and go as I like, thanks. So damn boring here I can't stand it for long anyways.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Come and go as you like? You're jeopardizing the task. Don't be such a child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't be such a child. Waitwaitwait... does that make you my mom? I never answered to Nyx. Think I'm about to start with you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Yeah? And just what do you think you're gonna do about it, there, Nemmie? Can't just have it out with me in the middle of your little Crossroads.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't do anything about it here. But might run into you out there, snooping around. And then you won't get any special treatment. Just another target.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh! Nemesis, how could you be so cruel? You're my sister! We don't have to fight! And besides, what makes you think you'd ever catch me? You're too slow.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You are required at your post, Nemesis. Or shall you take to openly defying me just as your sister does? I shudder to think what your mother would say of you two.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You have no right to invoke my mother. None of this would be happening had you fulfilled your role as Handmaiden, and warned her an attack was imminent.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Best watch your mouth or I shall seal it shut! You may continue dwelling on a past you were not witness to, or you may do what is expected of Nyx's daughters. Or whatever Eris is up to! Your choice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Insubordination, dereliction of duty, falsehood, intimidation, obstinance, simply setting a negative example for the Shades... anything else, would you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Indulgence, callousness, negligence. Hecate, I... Headmistress, I accept your judgment, and I have no excuses for my deeds.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Well thank the Fates that you would spare me that, at least. Now then, with Nyx returned I ought petition her to take you back. Surely the House of Hades could use another hand?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Don't. You know as well as I my place is here. But I'm Retribution Incarnate... I'm not here only to stand guard.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Clearly. Much retribution is to come, both above and below in these changing times... and you shall administer it in accordance with Night's will, when and wheresoever you see fit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Retribution whenever and wherever I see fit... you're letting me proceed? What have I done to deserve that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis, you hold such answers, not I. Now go, and see to our collective charge that time flows freely forth.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Zzzzz, zzzz...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at him, all peaceful. Not a care in the world. Could you imagine?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's beautiful. He has this wisdom about him, even asleep. Nyx must have taught you all so much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not really. Hypnos served in the House of Hades under Mother's care. Ran into him now and then, though. He ever wakes up, hope I can be there when you finally meet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I know these things, kid. As mortal lives go, yours was in one of the bottom categories. A lot shorter and more miserable than average. So you're making up for it a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, so you're saying, Goddess, that I'm to have a better life after death because my life before death was in one of the bottom categories according to some ranking system that you have... hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: That's right. Don't waste it, don't get greedy, you'll be fine. Just keep doing what you're doing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: But... I'm so fortunate to be here with you all. A short life in exchange for a good eternity, by your reasoning that isn't a fair trade. What have I done to deserve this...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: You haven't asked too many questions, for one thing. And don't take eternity for granted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And there she is! Shouldn't you be halfway down to Tartarus by now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Princess. Thank the Fates you're well. Nemesis and I, we were just catching up. She's told me a lot about you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, great. I can imagine! Though, you never told me about your brother, Nemesis...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got a lot of brothers. What's to tell? Never a good sign when he shows up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...It's my job, Moros. Simple as that. No one else wants to do it. No one else can do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: But the retribution you see fit to dole out... how can you be so certain that decisions about such consequences are yours alone? The Fates have quite a bit of say in it as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: What, you think I'm just another mindless puppet our sisters are pushing around? Come on! Their prophecies are vague because they don't really know what's coming.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Even if that were true, the burden of choosing who deserves what... I wouldn't want to bear it on my own. Otherwise my judgment would have to be perfect at all times; yet we are fallible beings.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Quite a predicament we find ourselves in, Sister. Certainly not one I could have anticipated when first I arrived. How odd... so what are we to do? It's not my wish to interfere.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry about that, worry about keeping your mouth shut when she's walking up on us. All casual like she doesn't know what's going on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait, what is going on? What are you two talking about, if I may ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: Talking about you, Princess, though it's nothing you need to worry your pretty little head about. You've got enough going on, and... we can sort this out ourselves. If you'll excuse us?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait. No, I would prefer to be forthright. Princess, the issue is we each developed certain feelings for you that, in some cases, can manifest as the kind of envy that Nemesis is committed to punishing. An outcome we are trying to avoid.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh! I... I see. Thank you for letting me know. I would never want to hurt either of you. Well maybe you, Nemesis, but only when we spar. How do we resolve this? Our feelings mustn't get in our way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: They won't get in our way. And besides, you're busy, not like you were going to devote yourself to one of us, body and soul. Like some mortal that doesn't know any better.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Certainly not... why should I or any of us have to choose? We're gods. If you wish to know me better, wonderful. But please accept that I won't let myself be bound to anyone, nor would I expect any different from you. Are we clear?
